title: Profiling, Allocator Performance, and Hardening · Failure Case: Allocator Selection
summary: A low-latency gateway reduces allocations but increases peak memory, fragmentation, and tail pauses under changed traffic. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem allocator selection increment in measured allocator optimization report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.
Relate object layout, traversal order, working set, cache behavior, false sharing, and measured latency.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Allocator Selection · Theory Allocator Selection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 6775. Produce bounded C evidence to compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compare system allocator, mimalloc, jemalloc, arenas, and pools using representative workloads and operational constraints.

steps.title: Build Allocator Selection evidence · Theory Allocator Selection
steps.instruction:

It must compare live, reserved, and peak memory before optimizing, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails.
